change_comments.message:
some suggestions
did not review testware yet as there could be some significant prod code changes needed
I will push a patch with changes DataMigrationSpec as i wanted to do some experimentation and needed quiet a few updates. Just 2 speed things up...

some legacy issues in the production code
testware needs a little more work
all good, missed one previous comment and some very minor Groovy improvements

NB: when last parameter doesn't fit I always start put all parameters on a line each
(jut a preference thing)
remove 'toUpdate' is superfluous, obvious from the method name

two verbs is a bit strange set AND update ?!
I think it could just be 'update'
not sure if 'new' is really need, pretty clear from method context?
it OK to use 'protected' for testing purposes, wouldn't be the first time

maybe keep the classname more generic but make the method name more specific

DataMigration.migrateInventoryToModel....


I think we need to include the model version target in the method name for clarity and future reference and/or deciding on deprecation

'i' and 'end' make more readable using
'batchStart' and ''batchEnd'  ?

maybe extract out variable for readability and clarify context context:
valueFromOldModel = handle.getCompositeState().getCmHandleState().name()

why can we not inject the bean here as normal?!
If we cannot inject it does/should it need to be a bean at all?
since this cannot really happen and you are catching ALL exceptions on the next line I dont think this is needed
NB: one line (no limits in Groovy and I prefer single lines unless they are really way too long

dont think any logging assert are needed (or done currently) so this is not needed
Loggingn asserts are really only doen as a last resort if theer are no real results or interactions to be checked

no expectation ins slogans
Test 1: 'CM Handle migration.'
Test 2: 'CM Handle migration with exception for one cm handle.'
Test 3: 'CM Handle migration without any cm handles'
what is grouped, how is that asserted?!

why SIX i think the test can be done with just 2 (maybe 3) cm handles?
Less maintanence!
is this really covering a branch?, would go agaist the clean code rule of no empty check before (simple) iterations....

no short names
(that was the rule I meant to add in this morning review of common mishaps)

not sure why you are mocking basic data holders?!
You can use groovy map-constructors instead to only define the properties you need.
I am sure we have test doing exactly this all over our testware for NcmpServiceCmHandle and otehr types....
have some doubts about the whole reflection bit, need to discuss

don't use word like Map or List in instance names
for Map I usually do somethign like
valuePerCmHadnleId
but cmHandleIdToValue is ok too

why i list of maps with only one entry each, you can just all add them in oen map ?!
Also did you not create  a dedicated 'record' instead of using a Map or did that cause problems here?!

org.onap.cps.utils.JsonObjectMapper (we already have a few variations here)

The CpsServicesBundle is the wrapper aroud the multipke services.
The CpsServicesConfig is the place you tell Spring how to create that wrapper bean.
Technically you can do in one class but CpsServicesBundle will no longer be a pure object and will be harder to re-use in non-Spring contexts if that is something we want to do.
It seems to be fairly common practice to separate the configuration or definition of beans from the actual type to instantiate.
Legaci issue: i think it makes more sense to swap those parameters so the signature looks like this: updateCmHandleFields(fieldName, newVauePerCmHandleId)
realised later that the same applies here as in code, please use string literals unless you are dealing with really complex variables
we re going to check this values below so use: 'my id' or 'ch-1' or similar
'my field name' I also prefer spaces to not give the impression these strings are being validated and for more natural reading
'new value' for best meaning (and we will chech it below)
use string literals, recommended values detailed in comment above

Groovify like so:

But since you know all the valued would prefer this

1 * objectUnderTest.updateCmHandleFields([ch-1:'my new value'],'my field')

(notice how ch-1 does even need to be inside ' ' that works as long as there are no spaces or other special tokens (I think the dash is ok)

For future reference if you need to use variables you need to wrap the the key(s) inside () so NOT to interpret as string literals:

1 * objectUnderTest.updateCmHandleFields([(cmHandleId):fieldValue], fieldName)

Google groovy maps for more details

like above this can be much simpler in Groovy:
1 * objectUnderTest.updateCmHandleFields([cm-1:'READY',cm-2:'DELETING'],'cm-handle-state'
after writing some Groovy test always check for unnecessary " instead of ' 😁

why collection data if you just need to check its empty?!

I think you can even do this in Groovy for this case:
 1 * mockInventoryPersistence.bulkUpdateCmHandleStates([])
 
 [] meaning its an empty collection (dont care about the type)
no idea what this tets is about, it seems unfinsihed
no need for this not null check since you test its properties below
always precede all assertions with 'assert' keyword
